title: Benchright Round End Table
summary: A round-topped end table with a 2x3-legged base and a mitered 1x3 lower shelf skirt — the round-top companion to this catalog's square Benchwright pieces.
description:
The round top is built from straight 1x4 boards rather than a single round panel — cut and arrange them so the assembled rectangle covers a 24" circle, then trace and cut the circle out once they're joined. That's a far more accessible way to get a round top than trying to source or glue up a round blank, and it lets you use the same straight-board techniques as the rest of the piece.

The lower shelf gets a mitered 1x3 skirt around its base, purely decorative but worth taking slowly since 45° miters on eight separate small pieces leave a lot of chances for a gap to show. Dry-fit the whole skirt around the shelf before gluing anything permanently.

tools.note: Cutting the round tabletop from the assembled square panel.

materials.name: Pine, 2x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs. Rip from a 2x6 if 2x3 isn't stocked locally.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Or substitute a pre-cut 24" round tabletop blank.

cutList.note: Both ends beveled 45°, not parallel, long point to long point.

steps.title: Build the bottom shelf
steps.body: Join the bottom shelf boards edge to edge, using either a pocket-hole jig or a wood block underneath for support while fastening.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Add the side and top aprons
steps.body: Attach the mitered shelf skirt pieces around the base of the shelf, dry-fitting the whole ring before committing to glue. Then add the top aprons connecting the legs — 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws work well here. To attach the top with pocket holes later, drill the top-facing pocket holes into the aprons now, while they're still accessible.

steps.title: Assemble and cut the round top
steps.body: Join the 1x4 tabletop boards edge to edge into a full panel. Mark a 24"-diameter circle centered on the panel and cut it out with a jigsaw, then sand the edge smooth and even.

steps.title: Attach the top to the base
steps.body: Attach the round top to the base, either through the predrilled apron pocket holes from underneath, or with decorative bolts or nails through the top.

images.alt: A round-topped end table with a mitered lower shelf skirt
